_comment: Optional airband receiver config for maia-httpd. Copy to /root/airband.json on the Pluto. If absent, maia-httpd uses its built-in defaults, now identical to this file (including gain_db 12). samp_rate MUST be the value the channelizer was built for (audio rate = samp_rate / 128 / 5 = 21875 sps at 14 MHz). channels_hz: up to 21 absolute channel centers, all within center_hz +/- samp_rate/2. gain: default fixed manual 12 dB, tuned for an external LNA ahead of the Pluto. The receiver is INTERNAL-noise-limited (the ch11 audio floor is identical with the antenna or a 50 ohm load, within 0.4 dB, and rises only ~1 dB per +6 dB of gain), so the floor is ADC quantization / conducted comb downstream of the gain. At 0 dB the wanted signal sits AT that floor (a controlled sweep on the continuous 118.050 AWOS carrier measured audio SNR ~1 dB at 0 dB, ~10-12 dB by 6-12 dB, plateauing to 42 dB); ~12 dB is the minimum that lifts voice clear of quantization and does not clip the wideband ADC (0 %, ~7 dB headroom with the LNA). The external LNA still matters for SFDR (low system NF; lets the internal stage -- the dominant comb/noise generator -- run lower) but does NOT replace the ~12 dB internal gain. On a BARE front end (no external LNA) raise gain_db toward the 48 dB clipping knee. See firmware/diagnostics/ and SPUR-INVESTIGATION.md.
